projectDescription: An AI coding agent for VS Code that automates the full embedded firmware inner loop — scaffold, build, flash, test, observe, and fix — for Espressif ESP and Nordic nRF platforms.
keyFeatures: Full embedded inner loop automation: scaffold, build, flash, test, observe, fix
Dynamic, on-demand knowledge loading — loads only the modules relevant to the active task
Human-curated firmware expertise authored by senior IoT engineers, not AI-generated
Hardware-in-the-loop instrumentation: RTT/UART log capture, J-Link multi-device control
Zero-friction free tier — no API key, account, or credit card required to start
Bring-your-own-key support for any OpenAI-compatible provider (cloud or local)
Support for Nordic nRF52/53/54 (NCS/Zephyr) and Espressif ESP32/S3/C6 (ESP-IDF)
BLE and Wi-Fi workflow support with roadmap for Thread, Matter, and LTE-M
Open-source Apache 2.0 license, built on the Cline foundation
Open benchmark (IoT-FirmwareDebugBench v0.1): 5/6 vs 3/6 bugs at 3.8x fewer tokens vs Claude Code
publicApiSurface: VS Code Settings: API endpoint URL (any OpenAI-compatible endpoint)
VS Code Settings: API key configuration
VS Code Settings: model name (e.g. anthropic/claude-haiku-4.5)
VS Code Settings: prompt caching toggle
Free tier: managed model inference with token-metered usage and balance indicator
Workflows: prototype, build/flash/debug, add a feature, test & validate
Hardware support: J-Link, RTT/UART capture, serial monitor, multi-device correlation
Local model support: Ollama, LM Studio, llama.cpp (OpenAI-compatible endpoint)
